mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures consumer demand momentum. Persistent increases in real retail sales translate into higher expected corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS growth are not positive in more than 50% of months, or mean return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below its 12-month trailing maximum by more than 0.5 percentage points signals tightening labor market conditions that precede wage-driven consumption and profit growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls more than 0.5pp below its 12-month trailing maximum
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or insignificantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) in the six months following trigger activation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) combined with a federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 0.25 percent indicates a production recovery supported by accommodative monetary policy. This interaction accelerates earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and FEDFUNDS does not significantly predict 20-day returns (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression, or the sign flips negative over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when the federal funds rate is below 0.25 percent, reflecting an early-cycle recovery dynamic where supply gains outpace inflationary drag.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth coincides with negative SPX 60-day returns in periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or the hypothesis fails if Rank IC < 0.03 in this regime over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 1.5 million units annualized signals robust construction activity and durable goods demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice cyclical earnings.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when HOUST > 1500, or the mean return is below zero (t-test p < 0.05 for negativity).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decreases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) reduce the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows and stimulate credit expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month declines in FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) signal broad demand expansion with pricing power, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days because the interaction amplifies earnings expectations more than either variable alone.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and CPIAUCSL does not significantly predict 5-day returns (F-test p > 0.05 for interaction term), or the signal generates negative mean returns over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) above 2 percent annualized signals aggregate demand outstripping supply constraints, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations reset higher.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) following quarters when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized.
